verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. As an inner-leaflet-anchored kinase it is not directly accessible from the extracellular face under baseline conditions. However, PMID 39289808 explicitly identifies LYN as a 'plasma-membrane-localized nonreceptor tyrosine kinase' upregulated on the trophoblast plasma membrane during syncytialization — a tissue/developmental-state-restricted context. PMID 11913944 and PMID 22488955 document LYN association with plasma membrane lipid rafts/microdomains in B cells and neutrophils, consistent with regulated PM association. The dominant localization is inner-leaflet/cytoplasmic, but documented PM minority pool and cell-type-restricted surface enrichment (trophoblast syncytialization, B-cell lipid rafts) support a contextual call via dual_localization rather than a strict no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: LYN is canonically inner-leaflet anchored (myristoylated/palmitoylated); extracellular accessibility of the protein body itself is not established — the PM association is cytoplasmic-face. The trophoblast surface proteomics (PMID 39289808) may reflect detection of the cytoplasmic-face pool.
